semanticCore: Importunity in begging, persistent asking, pressing with urgency, or covering with a blanket
quranicRelevance: Do not beg importunately (Ilhafan - 'la yas'aloona al-nasa ilhafan').

keyDerivations.meaning: Importunity, persistence in begging, pressing, or demanding with urgency

figurativeOrRareUsages: يَحْسَبُهُمُ الْجَاهِلُ أَغْنِيَاءَ مِنَ التَّعَفُّفِ تَعْرِفُهُمْ بِسِيمَاهُمْ لَا يَسْأَلُونَ النَّاسَ إِلْحَافًا (2:273): The ignorant thinks them rich because of their modesty. You know them by their mark: they do not beg of people importunately.
discardedNoise: Lehaf (blanket/coverlet) - though derived from wrapping around, not the conversational begging focus here.
summaryEn: The root denotes wrapping or pressing urgently (Lahf), praising the dignified poor who refuse to beg persistently or demand money from others.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الإلحاح الشديد في الطلب والملازمة والإصرار في السؤال حتى كأنه يلتف بالمسؤول (لحف)، مادحاً الفقراء المتعففين الذين لا يلحفون في السؤال.
rawText: لِحَافٌ : see what follows. مِلْحَفَةٌ A مُلَآءَة that is سُمُط [not lined, nor stuffed]: if lined or stuffed, the vulgar also call it by this name, but the Arabs do not know this: (L, TA:) and the same applies to the 🡻لِحَاف: Az says, that لِحَافٌ and مِلْحَفٌ mean the same: like إِزَارٌ and مِئْزَرٌ, and قِرَامٌ and مِقْرَمٌ; and sometimes one says مِقْرَمَةٌ and مِلْحَفَةٌ; and it is the same whether the garment be سُمُط or lined. (TA.) He says also, [in another place,] that the Arabs apply the terms 🡻لِحَافٌ and مِلْحَفَةٌ to A night-wrapper (إِزَارُ لَيْلِ) if it be طَاقٌ وَاحِدٌ [a single piece of stuff; i. e. not double, not lined nor faced, nor stuffed]. (TA in art. سمط.) === See إِزَازٌ.
